MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Feb-14-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. DANIEL QUALLS, Esq of O'Hagan Meyer LLP (Tel: 415-578-6900) for Plaintiff.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT KONIKU, INC's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's motion to vacate the default and default judgment is denied.

After the court struck defendant's answer and it failed to obtain counsel and file a proper response to the complaint, there was nothing preventing plaintiff from requesting and the clerk entering a default.

Plaintiff had the right to dismiss its cross-complaint and defendant's answer did not plead a viable claim. As this court previously ruled, affirmative relief may not be sought in the answer. (Aetna Casualty & Surety Co. v. Humboldt Loaders, Inc. (1988) 202 Cal.App.3d 921, 927.) Defendant's citation to Gray v. Superior Court (1997) 52 Cal.App.4th 165, 174 is not persuasive because that case holds that affirmative relief may be requested in a partition action answer per statute and this is not a partition action. The general rule of Aetna applies.

Plaintiff did not waive the right to seek a default by requesting it months after the court struck defendant's answer. CRC 3.110(g) provides: "If a responsive pleading is not served within the time limits specified in this rule and no extension of time has been granted, the plaintiff must file a request for entry of default within 10 days after the time for service has elapsed. The court may issue an order to show cause why sanctions should not be imposed if the plaintiff fails to timely file the request for the entry of default." This rule is to encourage timely disposition of cases, not prevent plaintiffs from obtaining tardy defaults. An OSC and monetary sanctions is generally the penalty for tardy defaults. Defendant's position that plaintiff "waived" the right to obtain a default fails. Under that theory, if the plaintiff misses the 10-day deadline (and purportedly cannot obtain a default) and the defendant never appears, then the only way the case could be resolved would be by dismissal-an untenable result. Baird v. Smith (1932) 216 Cal. 408 simply holds that a plaintiff's failure to obtain a default is an implied grant of further time for the defendant to respond. It does not hold that a plaintiff's time to obtain a default is somehow cut off when not sought with alacrity.

Defendant fails to demonstrate that it is entitled to equitable or CCP 473 relief. This motion was not brought within a reasonable time and defendant fails to demonstrate excusable neglect.

The court does not rule on whether the judgment is defective. A motion attacking the judgment should be made to the hearing officer that issued the judgment (Judge Maria Evangelista in department 505).

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Dec-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Daniel Qualls, Esq. 628-626-6906 Barber Ranen LLP for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KONIKU, INC.'s Motion To Foreclose Improper Dismissal Of Oriza's Cross-Complaint Or, In The Alternative, To Reinstate Orizas Cross-Complaint.

Koniku, Inc.'s "motion to foreclose improper dismissal of Oriza's cross-complaint or, in the alternative, to reinstate Oriza's cross-complaint" is denied. The at-issue dismissal was filed, and entered by a court clerk, on October 31, 2023. Koniku says that was improper because its answer to the cross-complaint pled "new matter seeking affirmative relief," so its consent to dismissal was required. (Memo. 4:20-26.) However, the modern rule is that affirmative relief may not be sought in an answer. (CCP 431.30(c); Aetna Casualty & Surety Co. v. Humboldt Loaders, Inc. (1988) 202 Cal.App.3d 921, 927.) Given that dispositive law, the parties' other arguments need not be reached.

Order is signed.
